> [!WARNING]
> This document is roadmap or specification material. It may describe planned, experimental, or partially implemented behavior rather than current DataLinq behavior.
# Specification: Generated File Headers and Stamping

**Status:** Draft implementation plan.
**Goal:** Every C# model and database file produced by DataLinq tooling should clearly say that it is generated by DataLinq and should not be edited by hand. The CLI should also offer an opt-in switch that includes the DataLinq CLI version and generation timestamp in generated file headers.

## Executive Position

This is worth doing, but the timestamp part must be opt-in. A generated-file warning is cheap and unambiguously helpful. It prevents accidental edits, lets IDEs and analyzers recognize generated code, and gives users a clean answer when they wonder whether a file belongs to them or to DataLinq.

Putting a date in every generated file by default would be a mistake. It turns every regeneration into a diff even when the schema did not change. That makes reviews worse, dirty working trees noisier, and deterministic build expectations weaker. The sane split is:

- Always emit a stable generated-file banner.
- Always include the C# `<auto-generated />` marker in generated C# files.
- Let CLI users opt in to version and timestamp stamping when traceability matters more than clean diffs.
- Keep source-generator output deterministic. Do not include the current date in compiler-generated sources.

## Current Code Audit

### CLI `create-models`

`src/DataLinq.CLI/Program.cs` defines `CreateModelsOptions` with `--skip-source` and `--overwrite-types`. It builds `ModelGeneratorOptions` and passes them to `src/DataLinq.Tools/ModelGenerator.cs`.

`ModelGenerator.CreateModels(...)` reads provider metadata, optionally merges existing source model metadata, creates `ModelFileFactoryOptions`, then writes every generated file directly with `File.WriteAllText(...)`.

`src/DataLinq.SharedCore/Factories/Models/ModelFileFactory.cs` is the CLI C# file renderer:

- `CreateModelFiles(DatabaseDefinition database)` returns the database model file as `{database.CsType.Name}.cs`.
- It then returns one file per non-stub table model.
- `GetFilePath(...)` can place table and view files under `Tables/` and `Views/`.
- `FileHeader(...)` currently emits only `using` statements and the namespace.

There is no generated-file marker today.

### Source Generator Output

`src/DataLinq.Generators/ModelGenerator.cs` constructs `GeneratorFileFactory` and calls `context.AddSource(...)` for every generated file.

`src/DataLinq.SharedCore/Factories/Generator/GeneratorFileFactory.cs` renders:

- one generated model implementation file per non-stub table
- one `{database.CsType.Name}.DataLinqMetadata.cs` bootstrap file when at least one table is generated

Its `FileHeader(...)` also currently emits only `using` statements and the namespace.

Source-generator output is compiler-owned and normally not edited by hand, but the generated source still benefits from `<auto-generated />` for analyzers and from a clear DataLinq banner when users inspect generated files in IDEs.

### Existing Version Surface

`DataLinq.CLI` is packaged as a .NET tool and references `MinVer`. `CommandLineParser` already exposes the built-in `version` command. `docs/CLI Documentation.md` documents that command as parser/tool metadata.

For header stamping, the CLI should read its own assembly metadata, preferably `AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute`, falling back to `AssemblyName.Version` and then `unknown`.

## Desired Header Policy

### Stable Header

Every generated C# file should start with a stable banner before any `using`, namespace, or generated declaration:

```csharp
// <auto-generated />
// This file was generated by DataLinq. Do not edit this file directly.
```

That marker should be present in:

- CLI-generated database model files, such as `MyDatabase.cs`
- CLI-generated table/view model files, including files under `Tables/` and `Views/`
- source-generator table model implementation files
- source-generator database metadata bootstrap files, such as `MyDatabase.DataLinqMetadata.cs`

The wording should be short and boring. This is not the place for command help, config paths, or regeneration instructions that might drift out of date.

### Optional CLI Stamp

`datalinq create-models` should have an optional switch that adds version and timestamp lines to the stable banner. Recommended name:

```text
--stamp-generated-header
```

Generated CLI output with the switch enabled:

```csharp
// <auto-generated />
// This file was generated by DataLinq. Do not edit this file directly.
// DataLinq CLI version: 1.2.3+abcdef
// Generated at: 2026-05-14T16:35:10.0000000Z
```

Rules:

- Use UTC.
- Use round-trip ISO 8601 formatting, `DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToString("O", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)`.
- Keep the default unstamped for clean diffs.
- Do not include the local machine name, user name, absolute config path, or connection string.
- Do not include timestamps in source-generator output.

### Source Generator Determinism

The source generator should emit the stable banner only:

```csharp
// <auto-generated />
// This file was generated by DataLinq. Do not edit this file directly.
```

It should not include generation time. Including the generator assembly version is defensible because it is deterministic for a package reference, but it is not required for this slice and should not be mixed with the CLI version switch. If generator-version stamping is desired later, expose it as a separate MSBuild/analyzer-config option and keep timestamps out.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add a Shared Header Value Object

Add a small shared type near the file factories, for example in `DataLinq.SharedCore/Factories/GeneratedFileHeaderOptions.cs`:

```csharp
public sealed class GeneratedFileHeaderOptions
{
    public static GeneratedFileHeaderOptions Stable { get; } = new();

    public string ToolName { get; init; } = "DataLinq";
    public string? ToolVersion { get; init; }
    public DateTimeOffset? GeneratedAtUtc { get; init; }
}
```

The type should not call the clock itself. Factories should only render values they are given. That keeps tests deterministic and keeps CLI-specific version logic out of shared rendering code.

### 2. Add Factory Options

Extend:

- `ModelFileFactoryOptions`
- `GeneratorFileFactoryOptions`

with:

```csharp
public GeneratedFileHeaderOptions GeneratedFileHeader { get; set; } = GeneratedFileHeaderOptions.Stable;
```

Both factories should use a shared helper to render header lines before `using` statements. Do not duplicate header wording in two private `FileHeader(...)` implementations.

### 3. Update File Header Rendering

Change both `FileHeader(...)` methods so their first emitted lines are:

1. `// <auto-generated />`
2. `// This file was generated by DataLinq. Do not edit this file directly.`
3. optional version line
4. optional timestamp line
5. blank line
6. sorted `using` statements
7. namespace

The `<auto-generated />` marker should be the first non-empty trivia in the file. That is the safest convention for analyzers, generated-code detection, and IDE behavior.

### 4. Plumb CLI Stamp Option

Add to `CreateModelsOptions`:

```csharp
[Option("stamp-generated-header", Required = false, HelpText = "Include DataLinq CLI version and UTC generation time in generated model file headers.")]
public bool StampGeneratedHeader { get; set; }
```

Add matching state to `ModelGeneratorOptions`, ideally as a concrete `GeneratedFileHeaderOptions` rather than as two separate booleans.

In `Program.cs`, when `StampGeneratedHeader` is true:

- resolve CLI version from `typeof(Program).Assembly`
- capture one `DateTimeOffset.UtcNow` before generation begins
- pass both values down to `ModelGenerator`

Capturing the timestamp once is important. A single `create-models` run should not stamp slightly different times into each generated file.

### 5. Keep Source Generator Stable

In `src/DataLinq.Generators/ModelGenerator.cs`, pass the default stable header to `GeneratorFileFactory`. Do not read the clock from the generator.

This matters because source generator output participates in IDE and compiler incremental behavior. A changing timestamp would force needless regenerated source text and could make generated-code diffs useless when users inspect compiler output.

### 6. Update Tests

Add focused TUnit coverage:

- `ModelFileFactory` emits the stable header in the database file.
- `ModelFileFactory` emits the stable header in table/view model files.
- `ModelFileFactory` emits version and timestamp lines when header options include them.
- `ModelFileFactory` uses the same timestamp for all files when the caller supplies one shared value.
- `GeneratorFileFactory` emits the stable header in generated table files.
- `GeneratorFileFactory` emits the stable header in the database metadata bootstrap file.
- `GeneratorFileFactory` does not emit a timestamp by default.

Existing tests that parse generated content with Roslyn should continue to work because comments before `using` statements are legal C#.

If any tests assert exact generated file text, update expected baselines deliberately. Prefer asserting header presence plus specific generated body behavior instead of making every unrelated generation test brittle.

### 7. Update User-Facing CLI Docs After Implementation

After the code lands, update `docs/CLI Documentation.md`:

- add `--stamp-generated-header` to the `create-models` option list
- explain that generated files always include a non-edit warning
- explain that version/date stamping is opt-in because it changes output on every run

Do not document this in user-facing docs before implementation.

## Compatibility and Migration Notes

This change will alter generated source text even with default options because the stable banner becomes part of every generated file. That is acceptable, but it should be called out in release notes because users who commit generated models will see one broad first-time diff.

The optional timestamp switch should not be enabled by config migration or default templates. Users who want traceability can opt in explicitly.

Existing handwritten model files should not be modified in place unless `create-models` regenerates them. This plan does not require a standalone file-rewrite migration.

## Non-Goals

- Do not add timestamps to source-generator output.
- Do not add machine-specific or user-specific data to generated headers.
- Do not make generated files read-only on disk.
- Do not add long instructions or regeneration commands to every file header.
- Do not change SQL script headers in this slice. `SchemaDiffScriptGenerator` already has a script-specific warning, and SQL artifacts have a different review/execute workflow than C# model files.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All CLI-generated C# database and model files start with the stable DataLinq generated-file banner.
- All source-generator C# files start with the stable DataLinq generated-file banner.
- `datalinq create-models --stamp-generated-header` includes CLI version and one shared UTC generation timestamp in every generated file from that run.
- Default `datalinq create-models` output remains deterministic for unchanged metadata, aside from the one-time addition of the stable banner.
- Source-generator output remains deterministic and does not include the current date.
- Tests cover stable and stamped headers without relying on the real system clock.
